title: Demonstrate the basic ability to navigate a file system and execute programs through both an interface (eg. File Explorer) and a shell (eg. powershell/command prompt)
Use and understand the difference in primitive data types
Print to console
Read from console
Use and understand conditional statements (if/else)
Use and understand logical operators (and, or, not) *excludes xor
Use and understand various loops (while, do while, for, for each)
Create a flowchart showing at least decisions, processes, and looping.
Use and understand arithmetic operators (+, -, /, *, % (mod))
Use and understand basic math functions (round, floor, ceiling, pow)
Demonstrate the difference between passing parameters by value or by reference
Understand and use static variables
Demonstrate an understanding of scope
Demonstrate an understanding of ASCII character values
Read from file
Write to file
Use and understand functions (including void/subs)
Use and understand arrays (eg. single data type, fixed length, accessed by index)
Use and understand lists
Demonstrate event handling (eg. do something on click)
Create and understand a class diagram
Create a class and instantiate objects from it
Create and understand the purpose of accessor/mutator methods
Inherit from a class
Overload a function
Override a base class's method(s)
Use and understand object composition (one class using another)
Demonstrate an understanding of static methods vs. non-static methods
Use and understand exception handling (try/catch)
Create a basic wireframe
Demonstrate an understanding of the phases of the Software Development Life Cycle
Demonstrate an understanding of at least 3 different development methodologies (waterfall, agile, prototyping)
Use and understand the basics of document markup (Eg. HTML) by creating a page (or pages) that contain (at least) a paragraph, an image, a link, a list, a table, and multiple heading levels.
Use and understand CSS and basic css selectors (tag, id, class, attribute)
Use CSS to create a responsive webpage that displays a page differently based on device width.
Manipulate a document with scripting (use JS to update the DOM)
Make an api request and process the data (eg. JS Fetch)
Use and understand polymorphism
Query a database table (Simple SELECT)
Query a database table with a restricted result set (WHERE)
Query multiple database tables to retrieve specific records (JOIN)
Query a database and group the results
Create a database table
Update a row (or rows) in a database table based on a condition
Insert a row in a database table
create an index on a table
Create and execute a database procedure
Create and query a database view
Create an ER diagram that clearly shows the relationship between tables
Use a programming language to connect to a database, query it, and then process the results
Create a json file containing at least 1 nested object, 1 array, a string and a numeric type
Demonstrate the ability to work from a Project Management System (Eg. Trello or GitLab) by managing tasks and updating cards.
Demonstrate understanding of code commenting through use of line comments, block comments, intellisense/Javadoc comments
Demonstrate ability to use a local repository (git)
Demonstrate ability to commit code and push it to a remote repository
Demonstrate ability to pull code
Demonstrate ability to clone a repository
Demonstrate ability to merge code
Demonstrate ability to revert code
Create use case scenarios for an application
Demonstrate ability to write and run unit tests
Demonstrate an understanding of the importance of ergonomics (posture, screen position, seating)
Demonstrate the ability to identify a phishing scam
Debugging: Add breakpoints and use them in a program
Debugging: Run the program in debug mode
Debugging: Step through the code line by line
Debugging: Analyze variables in the debugging panel
Linux: Demonstrate the ability to navigate the file system from the command line (cd)
Linux: Demonstrate the ability to list the content of a folder
Linux: Demonstrate the ability to add/remove files
Linux: Demonstrate the ability to add/remove folders
Linux: Demonstrate the ability to view help documentation on a command
Linux: Demonstrate the ability to use the grep command
Linux: Demonstrate the ability to use the head/tail commands
Linux: Demonstrate the ability to use the locate command
Linux: Demonstrate the ability to install a program from the command line
